scope: PR #135 (fix/openai-compat-cc-quality) — commit 0ad0345e, 5 files / 214 insertions / 36 deletions, branched off main@afc2c503
validation_gaps: No service-layer end-to-end test verifies that OpenAIForwardResult.StopReason / IncompleteReason are populated correctly along the buffered (non-streaming) path; only apicompat-level state.StopReason is asserted (TestStreamingIncompleteNonBudgetReason).
No service-layer test verifies the openai_messages.completed access log fields (zap.Info output is not captured by any zaptest observer in this PR).

findings.description: stop_reason mapping fix lives in an upstream-shaped file (apicompat package was introduced by upstream commit ff1f1149 by alfadb). The change is a clear protocol-correctness bug fix (every 'incomplete' upstream terminal must surface as Anthropic stop_reason='max_tokens', not silently 'end_turn'), not a TokenKey-only feature, so it is a strong candidate for an upstream PR back to Wei-Shaw/sub2api. Without sending it upstream, every future upstream merge will replay the original buggy logic and we'll have to re-apply the fix (or worse, lose it during a merge conflict resolution). Same situation as the US-027 family of fixes (922dd169, b14be823, 9f5e37ac).
findings.suggested_fix: After this PR lands, open a sibling PR against Wei-Shaw/sub2api with just the responsesStatusToAnthropicStopReason + resToAnthHandleCompleted change; track the upstream PR URL in a follow-up issue so we can close the divergence loop on the next merge/upstream-* PR.

findings.description: OpenAIForwardResult gained two new fields (StopReason, IncompleteReason) populated in two places: handleAnthropicBufferedStreamingResponse (line ~330) and handleAnthropicStreamingResponse (line ~552). Only the streaming path's apicompat-level state is unit-tested (TestStreamingIncompleteNonBudgetReason). The plumbing 'state.StopReason → result.StopReason' in the streaming path and 'finalResponse.IncompleteDetails → result.IncompleteReason' in the buffered path have no test. If a future refactor flips one of those wires (e.g. buffered path forgets to propagate IncompleteReason), prod will silently lose the very signal this PR adds.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a service-level test using existing openai_gateway_record_usage_test.go scaffolding (mock httptest.Server returning an SSE stream that ends with response.incomplete + content_filter, plus a buffered-path equivalent). Assert the returned *OpenAIForwardResult has StopReason='max_tokens' and IncompleteReason='content_filter' on both paths. Costs ~50 LOC and closes the validation gap.

findings.description: `_ = details` is redundant in Go — unused function parameters do not produce compile errors or default lint warnings (only unused locals do). The line was added defensively when the reason-based dispatch was removed, but it does not buy anything and may confuse the next reader into thinking there's a hidden side-effect being suppressed.
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the `_ = details` line. Optionally replace with a comment like `// details no longer drives dispatch — every incomplete maps to max_tokens — but the parameter is kept so callers (and the streaming counterpart) keep their existing signature.` if the next reader could misread the intent.

findings.description: The new openai_messages.completed access log unconditionally emits `incomplete_reason:""` for every successful (non-incomplete) request. Most lines on prod will carry the empty value, adding visual noise to `docker logs tokenkey | grep openai_messages.completed` and slightly bloating log volume. Same applies to `reasoning_effort` when ReasoningEffort is nil — it emits empty string.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap both fields in `if v := …; v != "" { fields = append(fields, zap.String("incomplete_reason", v)) }`. Keeps the happy-path log line tighter while preserving the diagnostic signal on cutoffs.
